name: Embalse de Bembibre, Pozo de las Hoyas y Matavenero – desde San Facundo
region_intro: Hiking trails around Torre Del Bierzo traverse a diverse landscape within the El Bierzo region of León, Spain. The area is characterized by extensive forests of chestnut and oak trees, river valleys shaped by the Tremor and Boeza rivers, and fertile plains. Mountainous terrain, part of the Cordillera Cantábrica, features deep valleys and offers varied vistas. Old vineyards also contribute to the picturesque scenery on the hillsides. Best hiking trails around Torre Del Bierzo The most popular hiking route is Embalse de Bembibre, Pozo de las Hoyas y Matavenero – desde San Facundo, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route requires good physical condition and steady footing. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cañones del Górgora Trail, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This route features waterfalls and canyon sections near Monteal. Local hikers also love the Paseo de los Molinos Trail, a 3.3 miles (5.2 km) trail leading through river valleys and past historical mills, often completed in about 1 hour 26 minutes. Hiking around Torre Del Bierzo is defined by dense woodlands, river valleys, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding mountain excursions. The routes in Torre Del Bierzo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Torre Del Bierzo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Torre Del Bierzo?
A: Torre Del Bierzo offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy walks through river valleys to more demanding mountain excursions within the Cordillera Cantábrica.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Torre Del Bierzo has several easy trails. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050764726/paseo-de-los-molinos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo de los Molinos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.2 km path that winds through river valleys and past historical mills. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047153647/canones-del-gorgora-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañones del Górgora Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.8 km and features waterfalls near Monteal.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Torre Del Bierzo?
A: The region is characterized by extensive forests of chestnut and oak trees, offering vibrant colors, especially in autumn. You'll also encounter picturesque river valleys shaped by the Tremor and Boeza rivers, fertile plains, and old vineyards. The mountainous terrain provides impressive vistas and deep valleys.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047153647/canones-del-gorgora-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañones del Górgora Trail&lt;/a&gt; is known for its waterfalls and canyon sections near Monteal. The Paseo de los Molinos also features a scenic waterfall.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can be found along the hiking routes?
A: Torre Del Bierzo is rich in history. You can explore ancient pre-Roman settlements like the one on the Ruta del Castro Las Torcas, or discover significant petroglyphs on the Senda de los Petroglifos. The region also has a notable mining heritage, with vestiges like old coal washing facilities and abandoned operations visible on some trails, particularly in autumn. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/716062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Ferro (Iron Cross)&lt;/a&gt; is a significant landmark nearby.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, the Senda de los Fósiles is a circular route, approximately 5 kilometers long, that takes you to the Mina Boisán, an important paleontological site showcasing evidence of ancient tropical forests.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Torre Del Bierzo?
A: Autumn is particularly recommended for hiking in Torre Del Bierzo. During this season, the extensive chestnut and oak forests display vibrant colors, and the natural beauty blends well with the area's industrial mining heritage, which can be observed along some routes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options?
A: Many of the easier trails are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050764726/paseo-de-los-molinos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo de los Molinos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, with its gentle terrain and historical mills, is a great option for families looking for a pleasant walk.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Torre Del Bierzo?
A: The hiking trails in Torre Del Bierzo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of natural beauty with historical elements, and the variety of routes available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that offer impressive viewpoints?
A: Yes, the Ruta del Castro Las Torcas, a 7-kilometer trail leading to an ancient pre-Roman settlement, offers unparalleled views of the El Bierzo region from its promontory.

Q: What is the difficulty level of the trails in Torre Del Bierzo?
A: The trails in Torre Del Bierzo offer a range of difficulties. You'll find easy walks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050764726/paseo-de-los-molinos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo de los Molinos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, moderate routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047117229/embalse-de-bembibre-pozo-de-las-hoyas-y-matavenero-desde-san-facundo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Bembibre, Pozo de las Hoyas y Matavenero – desde San Facundo&lt;/a&gt;, and more challenging mountain excursions for experienced hikers.
tips: Beautiful views of the Bembibre reservoir from the path that runs parallel to the Ríal stream.
Without a doubt, a bridge where we can take a nice picture. This original wooden bridge allows us to cross the Ríal stream, near the Pozo de las Hoyas.
The Pozo de las Hoyas is located in a spectacular setting in the Bierzo region that is worth visiting. It is part of a well indicated route from San Facundo to Matavenero.

Curious village known for being an ecological village. Several people settled in this town and in the nearby Poibueno to create an ecological town or also called Ecovillage. Currently it has all the basic services, such as drinking water (supplied by mountain streams) and energy supply (mainly through solar panels), the population grew, creating a series of artisan and agricultural businesses such as a bakery or a small restaurant.

Since its recovery as an inhabited town, more than 30 children have been born, which is why the addition of the residents of Matavenero made it necessary to create a school, which they call free school.

The movement of motor vehicles is prohibited in the town

waypoints.name: Wooden Bridge over the Ríal Stream
